title: Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
Identify key diagnostic features of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
Apply clinical reasoning to Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia management
Recognize common pitfalls in Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia

sections.title: Overview of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
sections.content:
**Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia**

Bochdalek vs Morgagni hernia, pulmonary hypoplasia, persistent pulmonary hypertension

**Key Points:**
• Bochdalek (posterolateral, LEFT 85%) — most common; Morgagni (anterior, right)
• Failure of pleuroperitoneal membrane closure at 8-10 weeks
• Abdominal contents herniate into thorax → pulmonary hypoplasia + persistent pulmonary HTN

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Bochdalek (posterolateral, LEFT 85%) — most common; Morgagni (anterior, right)
• Failure of pleuroperitoneal membrane closure at 8-10 weeks

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Abdominal contents herniate into thorax → pulmonary hypoplasia + persistent pulmonary HTN
• Do NOT bag-mask ventilate (distends stomach in thorax)
• Immediate: intubate, OG tube to decompress stomach; Definitive: surgical repair after stabilization

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia:**

• Abdominal contents herniate into thorax → pulmonary hypoplasia + persistent pulmonary HTN
• Do NOT bag-mask ventilate (distends stomach in thorax)
• Immediate: intubate, OG tube to decompress stomach; Definitive: surgical repair after stabilization

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia?

options.text: Bochdalek (posterolateral, LEFT 85%) — most common; Morgagni (anterior, right)
options.explanation: Bochdalek (posterolateral, LEFT 85%) — most common; Morgagni (anterior, right)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia?

options.text: Immediate: intubate, OG tube to decompress stomach; Definitive: surgical repair after stabilization
options.explanation: Immediate: intubate, OG tube to decompress stomach; Definitive: surgical repair after stabilization

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Failure of pleuroperitoneal membrane closure at 8-10 weeks
options.explanation: Failure of pleuroperitoneal membrane closure at 8-10 weeks
